div.head: 259. Memorandum From the President’s Military Representative (Taylor) to the President’s Special Assistant for National Security Affairs (Bundy)Source: National Defense University, Taylor Papers, Laos-SEATO 62, T–641–71H. Secret.
div.p:
In the course of the meeting today of the 5412 Special Group, we discussed the items of business which should be taken up at the 9:30 meeting tomorrow on Laos.This and Document 260 are the only records found relating to the January 6 meeting of the 5412 Special Group. They appear to be the following:
a.
A presentation by State of the alternatives facing us in Laos: (1)
“Persuade” Phoumi to play ball by the technique suggested in the 5412 paper which you have seen.Document 260. (2)
Get rid of Phoumi. (3)
Desist in our efforts to form a Souvanna Phouma government.
b.
An estimate by CIA of the probable enemy reaction to a break-off in negotiations. c.
A JCS estimate of the increased military capabilities of the Royal Laotian Army supported by indigenous tribesmen.
You may want to work up a formal agenda based upon the foregoing.

signed.note: Printed from a copy that bears these typed initials.

postscript.p: Attached hereto is a round-up made by Lt. Commander Bagley of activities going on today at State and Defense.Document 258.
